source.title: Remembering to Forget: A Dual Role for Sleep Oscillations in Memory Consolidation and Forgetting

confidence_rationale: Langille 2019 argues that NREM and REM sleep serve complementary and sequential functions: NREM installs and edits engrams via slow oscillation + spindle + SWR triple-phase locking (hippocampus-to-cortex, installation direction), while REM stabilises and integrates them, and that the cyclical organisation of NREM-REM sleep is the mechanism ensuring bidirectionality. The paper explicitly proposes that the same sleep rhythms that consolidate also adaptively forget, and that NREM/REM cycling is necessary for both. This maps onto ARC-045: the SWS-direction (hippocampus-to-neocortex) installs schema structure; the REM hippocampal theta rhythm serves the waking/REM-direction (neocortex-to-hippocampus, pattern completion, prior-guided slot-filling). The sequential cycling is what enables iterative convergence of attribution mapping across multiple NREM-REM cycles. Confidence moderate because Langille does not directly test whether disrupting one direction impairs attribution mapping, and the review integrates evidence from multiple sources rather than providing primary data.

mapping.source_claim_statement: NREM sleep consolidates new memories into hippocampus and cortex via triple-phase locking of slow oscillation, spindle, and SWR (hippocampus-to-neocortex installation direction). REM hippocampal theta rhythm stabilises and integrates engrams (neocortex-influenced hippocampal processing direction). The NREM-REM cycling enables sequential lay-down (NREM) and stabilisation/integration (REM) -- a bidirectional process that simultaneously consolidates new and adaptively forgets older memories.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-045: The NREM-REM cycling described by Langille is the temporal implementation of the bidirectional hippocampus-neocortex architecture ARC-045 requires. NREM = SWS-direction flow (hippocampus installs attribution estimates into neocortical schema structure); REM = waking/REM-direction flow (neocortical schema influences hippocampal theta-driven processing, filling attribution slots with prior-guided estimation). Attribution mapping convergence across the night requires multiple NREM-REM cycles: each cycle is one iteration of the bidirectional update (NREM installs, REM integrates with neocortical prior). The cyclical architecture prevents either direction from dominating -- the convergence is iterative, not one-shot.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Langille's model integrates evidence from multiple sources but the specific schema-installation vs. slot-filling distinction within the NREM-REM cycle is an inferential mapping to ARC-045. The paper's primary focus is the consolidation/forgetting duality rather than schema-first, slot-filling-second temporal ordering. The mechanisms proposed for REM stabilisation are less well-evidenced than NREM installation in the primary literature.
mapping.source_context: Review integrating NREM and REM sleep literature. Proposes cellular mechanisms for sleep-dependent consolidation (calcium kinetics, consolidative signalling cascades) and adaptive forgetting. Frontiers in Cellular Neuroscience 2019.

confidence_components.notes: Solid review in Frontiers Cellular Neuroscience. Mapping fidelity good for the bidirectional NREM/REM cycling as implementation of ARC-045's convergence architecture. Transfer risk low-moderate: hippocampal-neocortical dialogue mechanism is general; the schema/attribution distinction within the cycling is the inferential step.
